title: UniSAFE final conference: Pathways for change: steps ahead
author: Linková, Marcela
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The session was presented by Marcela Linková; Co-Chair of ERA Action 5 on inclusive gender equality implementation, Silvia Rueda; Advisory Member in the Cabinet of the Spanish Minister of Science and Innovation, Director of the Women and Science Unit, Spanish Ministry of Science and Innovation, Lut Mergaert; Research Director and Senior Consultant at Yellow Windowat UniSAFE final conference in Namur, 22. November.   The session highlights the importance of integrating care into EU policy making on gender-based violence in Research and Innovation (R&I). It emphasizes the ambition to address gender-based violence within the European Research Area, covering issues like sexual harassment in Higher Education (HE) and R&I. Besdides, The session covers the EU's Gender Equality Strategy 2020-2025, ERA Policy Agenda 2022-2024, and Spain's efforts to support gender equality in research. It highlights the idea of "effective equality" and points out the Spanish Presidency's active role in contributing to the ERA Policy Agenda. Finally, the session shares insights from UniSAFE, revealing that gender-based violence is a widespread systemic problem. It emphasizs that this issue does not impact individuals in academia uniformly, with certain groups experiencing disproportionate suffering. Additionally, the presentation touched upon underexplored issues within the UniSAFE framework.
